verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) has no transmembrane region of its own but is the obligate non-covalent light chain of MHC class I heterotrimers, assembled intracellularly with the TM-anchored MHC-I heavy chain and co-trafficked as a stable complex to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. It is constitutively displayed on the extracellular face of nearly all nucleated cells. This is a canonical stable_complex_partner architecture: B2M is integrated into the complex during biogenesis in the ER, not recruited post-translationally from the extracellular milieu. It is essential for MHC-I surface expression and stability. The extracellular accessibility of B2M is exploited by bispecific and engineered therapeutic formats. This is not a transient non-covalent equilibrium binding from solution, but a co-assembled, co-trafficked obligate heterotrimeric complex.
